query:
Research the company "pepsico".

Research [COMPANY NAME] for: recent quarterly earnings reports or investor calls mentioning P&L gaps, margin pressure, or guidance revisions; announcement of new CEO, CFO, or Chief Commercial Officer appointments; press releases on new product launches, platform expansions, or market entries; production capacity constraints, supply chain challenges, or manufacturing bottlenecks; recent restructuring, cost-cutting initiatives, or strategic reviews; commentary on pricing actions, promotional effectiveness, or distribution channel changes; industry and company scale (B2B/B2C, vertical focus, revenue tier). Look for recent news (last 6-12 months) indicating strategic inflection points.

Focus on the last 6 months. Return a comprehensive summary with specific facts, numbers, and dates.
answer:
## Executive Summary

PepsiCo has entered 2026 undergoing a significant strategic inflection point characterized by a major C-suite reorganization, aggressive supply chain optimization, and a product portfolio pivot toward health and protein-focused offerings. Despite macroeconomic headwinds and margin compression in late 2025, the company reported a strong Q1 2026 with $19.44 billion in revenue, beating analyst expectations and demonstrating early success from its cost-cutting and productivity initiatives. To address historical manufacturing bottlenecks, PepsiCo is actively reducing its U.S. product SKUs by 20%, closing older facilities, and heavily investing in AI and digital twin technologies to unlock hidden capacity.

## Detailed Analysis

### Financial Performance, Margin Pressures, and Guidance
PepsiCo experienced margin compression at the end of 2025 before rebounding in early 2026. 
*   **Q4 2025 Trough:** Operating margins compressed to 14.1% in Q4 2025, which management attributed to typical seasonal patterns, while gross margins hit a trough of 53.7% [PepsiCo Q1 2026 Earnings: What $19.4B in Revenue Means for the Investment Case | TIKR.com](https://www.tikr.com/blog/pepsico-q1-2026-earnings-what-19-4b-in-revenue-means-for-the-investment-case). Despite this, Q4 2025 net income increased by 67% due to a "low base effect" from 2024 [PepsiCo, Inc. (PEP) stock analysis and forecast for 2026 - RoboForex](https://roboforex.com/beginners/analytics/forex-forecast/stocks/stocks-forecast-pepsico-pep/).
*   **Q1 2026 Recovery:** The company reported Q1 2026 revenue of $19.44 billion, exceeding analyst expectations of $18.94 billion [PepsiCo (PEP) Earnings: Latest Report, Earnings Call & Financials](https://public.com/stocks/pep/earnings). Operating margins expanded to 17.0% (up from 15.8% in Q1 2025), though some sources reported the figure at 16.5% [PepsiCo Reports Strong Earnings Growth in Q1 2026 | Value The Markets](https://www.valuethemarkets.com/news/pepsico-reports-strong-earnings-growth-in-q1-2026). Core operating margin expanded by approximately 10 basis points year-over-year [Earnings call transcript: PepsiCo exceeds Q1 2026 forecasts with strong revenue growth By Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-pepsico-exceeds-q1-2026-forecasts-with-strong-revenue-growth-93CH-4618306).
*   **Guidance:** PepsiCo reaffirmed its full-year 2026 organic revenue guidance of 2% to 4%, expecting growth toward the upper end of that range in the second half of the year [PepsiCo Announces Priorities to Enhance Shareholder Value and Provides Preliminary 2026 Financial Outlook](https://www.pepsico.com/newsroom/press-releases/2025/pepsico-announces-priorities-to-enhance-shareholder-value-and-provides-preliminary-2026-outlook). The company expects core EPS growth of 5% to 7% for fiscal 2026 [PepsiCo Reports Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Results](https://investors.pepsico.com/docs/pepsico-5v9wci20/media/Files/investors/q4-2025-earnings-release.pdf) and aims to deliver at least 100 basis points of core operating margin expansion in aggregate over the next three fiscal years [PepsiCo Announces Priorities to Enhance Shareholder Value and Provides Preliminary 2026 Financial Outlook](https://www.pepsico.com/newsroom/press-releases/2025/pepsico-announces-priorities-to-enhance-shareholder-value-and-provides-preliminary-2026-outlook).
*   **Macroeconomic Risks:** Management is monitoring potential margin pressures from commodity inflation driven by the Iran conflict, though 6-to-12-month hedging programs are mitigating near-term volatility [Pepsico (PEP) Earnings Dates, Call Summary & Reports - TipRanks.com](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/pep/earnings). Other monitored headwinds include SNAP benefit revisions and GLP-1 drug uncertainty [Pepsico (PEP) Earnings Dates, Call Summary & Reports - TipRanks.com](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/pep/earnings).

### Executive Leadership Restructuring
In late 2025, PepsiCo executed a major overhaul of its C-suite and regional leadership, while Ramon Laguarta maintained his role as Chairman and CEO [PepsiCo, Inc. (PEP) Leadership & Management Team Analysis - Simply Wall St](https://simplywall.st/stocks/us/food-beverage-tobacco/nasdaq-pep/pepsico/management).
*   **CFO:** Steve Schmitt (formerly CFO of Walmart U.S.) was appointed EVP and Chief Financial Officer effective November 10, 2025, succeeding Jamie Caulfield, who retired after 30 years and will remain in an advisory role until May 15, 2026 [PepsiCo Announces New Chief Financial Officer](https://www.pepsico.com/newsroom/press-releases/2025/pepsico-announces-new-chief-financial-officer) [PepsiCo Announces New Chief Financial Officer](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/pepsico-announces-new-chief-financial-officer-302579563.html).
*   **CCO:** Steven Williams transitioned from CEO of PepsiCo North America to the newly created role of EVP, Vice Chairman, Global Chief Commercial Officer, and Corporate Affairs, effective December 28, 2025 [PepsiCo announces organizational changes to accelerate growth](https://www.pepsico.com/newsroom/stories/2025/pepsico-announces-organizational-changes-to-accelerate-growth).
*   **Regional Leadership:** Ram Krishnan succeeded Williams as CEO of PepsiCo North America on December 28, 2025 [PepsiCo announces organizational changes to accelerate growth](https://www.pepsico.com/newsroom/stories/2025/pepsico-announces-organizational-changes-to-accelerate-growth). Athina Kanioura was appointed CEO of Latin America Foods (replacing the retiring Paula Santilli) while retaining her role as Chief Strategy & Transformation Officer [PepsiCo announces organizational changes to accelerate growth](https://www.pepsico.com/newsroom/stories/2025/pepsico-announces-organizational-changes-to-accelerate-growth) [PepsiCo Reshapes Global Leadership to Accelerate Growth and Advance 2030 Strategy | PotatoPro](https://www.potatopro.com/news/2026/pepsico-reshapes-global-leadership-accelerate-growth-and-advance-2030-strategy). 

### Supply Chain Optimization and Capacity Constraints
PepsiCo executives have acknowledged that the company fell behind in manufacturing and distribution capacity, with existing facilities lacking the bandwidth for future product diversity and volume [PepsiCo’s Supply Chain Journey to ‘Always Everywhere’ - Supply Chain Management Review](https://www.scmr.com/article/pepsicos-supply-chain-journey).
*   **Restructuring & Cost-Cutting:** To reduce operational complexity, PepsiCo closed three manufacturing plants and several production lines in 2025 [PepsiCo Unveils 2026 Growth Strategy: Focus on Affordability and Efficiency](https://source86.com/pepsico-2026-financial-outlook-strategy-update/). The company is also cutting nearly 20% of its U.S. product SKUs by early 2026 [PepsiCo cuts SKUs and bets on protein snacks in major 2026 strategy reset](https://www.bakeryandsnacks.com/Article/2026/03/24/pepsico-cuts-skus-and-bets-on-protein-snacks-in-major-2026-strategy-reset/). These productivity programs have already yielded unit cost reductions for PepsiCo Foods North America in Q1 2026 [PepsiCo (PEP) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript - The Globe and Mail](https://www.theglobeandmail.com/investing/markets/markets-news/motley/1347859/pepsico-pep-q1-2026-earnings-call-transcript/).
*   **Bottlenecks:** Decades-old facilities limit throughput, and manual handling/inventory tracking have hampered the fulfillment of new product lines [PepsiCo reimagines supply chain performance through digital twins and AI with Siemens | Digital Logistics](https://blogs.sw.siemens.com/digital-logistics/2026/01/14/pepsico-reimagines-supply-chain-performance-through-digital-twins-and-ai-with-siemens/) [How is Pepsi Transforming Logistics and Supply Chain Tech? | Jones Elite Logistics](https://www.joneselitelogistics.com/blog/how-is-pepsi-transforming-logistics-and-supply-chain-tech/). Copackers remain a critical bottleneck, requiring two-month production schedule locks [PepsiCo's Practical Application Of Supply Chain Resilience Strategies](https://www.forbes.com/sites/stevebanker/2016/10/01/pepsicos-practical-application-of-supply-chain-resilience-strategies/). Furthermore, water scarcity and weather disruptions (e.g., tornadoes) pose ongoing threats to physical networks [What is Growth Strategy and Future Prospects of PepsiCo Company? – PortersFiveForce.com](https://portersfiveforce.com/blogs/growth-strategy/pepsico) [PepsiCo reimagines supply chain performance through digital twins and AI with Siemens | Digital Logistics](https://blogs.sw.siemens.com/digital-logistics/2026/01/14/pepsico-reimagines-supply-chain-performance-through-digital-twins-and-ai-with-siemens/).
*   **Technological Interventions:** At CES 2026, PepsiCo announced a multi-year collaboration with Siemens and NVIDIA to implement digital twin technology and AI, which has already uncovered "hidden capacity" and increased throughput by 20% in initial deployments [PepsiCo Announces Industry-First AI and Digital Twin Collaboration with Siemens and NVIDIA](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/pepsico-announces-industry-first-ai-and-digital-twin-collaboration-with-siemens-and-nvidia-302653851.html) [PepsiCo to Get Ahead of Supply Chain Challenges With 3D Digital Twin Tech | Consumer Goods Technology](https://consumergoods.com/pepsico-get-ahead-supply-chain-challenges-3d-digital-twin-tech). On April 22, 2026, the company partnered with Google Cloud to use the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform for supply chain optimization [PepsiCo deepens AI capabilities with Google Cloud](https://www.pepsico.com/newsroom/press-releases/2026/pepsico-deepens-ai-capabilties-with-google-cloud).
*   **Capacity Expansion:** PepsiCo is investing $665 million in India through 2030 to expand food manufacturing capacity [PepsiCo to invest USD 665 million in India foods manufacturing by 2030 | PotatoPro](https://www.potatopro.com/news/2026/pepsico-invest-usd-665-million-india-foods-manufacturing-2030) and is expanding its fleet of Gatik autonomous trucks to manage regional network demands [PepsiCo expanding autonomous truck use in its supply chain | Supply Chain Dive](https://www.supplychaindive.com/news/pepsico-expanding-autonomous-truck-use-in-its-supply-chain/822403/).

### Product Innovation, Platform Expansions, and Market Entries
PepsiCo's 2026 product strategy heavily emphasizes protein, functional health, and experiential platforms.
*   **Protein & Functional Snacking:** Early 2026 saw the launch of Doritos Protein (10g protein per serving, with a 17g version planned) [Doritos® Launches Boldest Innovation Yet: Doritos® Protein](https://www.pepsico.com/newsroom/press-releases/2026/doritos-launches-boldest-innovation-doritos-protein). The company also unveiled a 2026 protein beverage range including Propel Clear Protein powder, reformulated Muscle Milk, and Starbucks Coffee + Protein [PepsiCo unveils science-led 2026 protein beverage range](https://www.newfoodmagazine.com/news/256467/pepsico-2026-protein-beverages-portfolio/). The 'Simply NKD' line of Cheetos and Doritos is expanding to offer simpler ingredients [PepsiCo 2026 Strategy: Growth, Plant Closures & New Doritos](https://source86.com/pepsico-2026-financial-outlook-strategy-update/).
*   **Beverage Expansions:** Following a successful limited run, Pepsi Prebiotic Cola launched nationwide in the U.S. on February 17, 2026 [Pepsi® Prebiotic Cola now available nationwide in stores and online](https://www.pepsico.com/newsroom/press-releases/2026/pepsi-prebiotic-cola-now-available-nationwide-in-store-and-online). Prebiotic soda brand Poppi entered the UK on February 26, 2026, marking its first international expansion [Why is PepsiCo Betting Big on Prebiotic Sodas Like Poppi](https://www.kavout.com/market-lens/why-is-pepsico-betting-big-on-prebiotic-sodas-like-poppi). Other launches include Adrenaline Rush energy drink in India [PepsiCo Taps India Energy Drink Demand With Adrenaline Rush Launch](https://simplywall.st/stocks/us/food-beverage-tobacco/nasdaq-pep/pepsico/news/pepsico-taps-india-energy-drink-demand-with-adrenaline-rush), 'Pepsi Fizz' in Mexico [PepsiCo News and Press Releases | PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news/pepsico/), and a planned 'Dirty Mountain Dew Cream Soda' [PepsiCo packing protein into new product launches](https://www.foodbusinessnews.net/articles/29150-pepsico-packing-protein-into-new-product-launches).
*   **New Platforms & Categories:** 
    *   *Foodservice/B2B:* In June 2026, Pepsi Global launched 'House of Treats,' an experiential beverage platform for Away From Home channels [Pepsi® Global launches Pepsi ‘House Of Treats’ a new Away From Home experiential beverage platform](https://www.pepsico.com/newsroom/press-releases/2026/pepsi-global-launches-pepsi-house-of-treats-a-new-away-from-home-experiential-beverage-platform).
    *   *Restaurants:* March 2026 marked the opening of 'Pilla Tortilla' in Spain, the first-ever Lay’s branded restaurant concept [PepsiCo launches “Pilla Tortilla,” the first ever Lay’s restaurant, opening in Spain](https://www.pepsico.com/newsroom/press-releases/2026/pepsico-launches-pilla-tortilla-the-first-ever-lays-restaurant-opening-in-spain).
    *   *New Food Categories:* PepsiCo entered the ready-to-heat soup category with Alvalle vegetable soups in Spain on February 19, 2026 [PepsiCo expands food portfolio with new Alvalle hot soups](https://www.pepsico.com/newsroom/press-releases/2026/pepsico-expands-food-portfolio-with-new-alvalle-hot-soups).
    *   *Creator-Led:* February 2026 saw the launch of 'Flavor Swap,' the company's first creator-led product line targeting Gen Z [PepsiCo’s first creator-led product launch reimagines chips for Gen Z](https://www.marketingdive.com/news/pepsicos-first-creator-led-product-launch-reimagines-chips-for-gen-z/812502/).

## Gaps and Limitations
Based on the provided research findings, the following requested information could not be addressed:
*   **Pricing and Promotions:** There is no specific commentary available regarding recent pricing actions, promotional effectiveness, or distribution channel changes (beyond the launch of the 'House of Treats' Away From Home platform).
*   **Industry and Company Scale:** While Q1 2026 revenue ($19.44 billion) indicates the company's massive revenue tier, the findings do not explicitly define PepsiCo's exact B2B vs. B2C revenue splits, vertical focus definitions, or broader industry market share metrics.
